title: Feedback & Notifications
intro: Every async action answers back through one of five channels: NotificationMessage toasts for outcomes, antd message pills for split-second acknowledgements, Modal.confirm for consent, Overlay for in-place blocking, and LoadingAnimation for first loads. Pick the channel by consequence, not by taste.

blocks.body:
Feedback is how the app answers the user after an action or while one is in flight. Excalibrr ships two thin wrappers over antd's notification API — `NotificationMessage` (titled success/error toasts) and `ErrorNotification` (form-validation toast) — and two loading primitives: `Overlay` (antd Spin around in-place content) and `LoadingAnimation` (Lottie illustration for first loads). Everything else comes straight from antd: `message` for pills, `Modal`/`Modal.confirm` for dialogs, `Alert` for persistent inline warnings.

Toasts confirm what already happened; modals gather consent before it happens; overlays and loading states cover the time in between. Never substitute one for another — a toast after a silent delete is not a confirmation flow.

blocks.title: NotificationMessage — success and error toasts
blocks.caption: Top-right toast stack. Error state (showErrorMessage=true) renders WarningFilled in --theme-error; success state (false) renders CheckCircleFilled in --theme-success. Both auto-dismiss after antd's default 4.5s.

blocks.title: NotificationMessage(message, description, showErrorMessage)
blocks.intro: A function call, not a component — invoke it from event handlers. It wraps `notification.open` and adds the themed status icon.

rows.notes: Toast title. Short outcome statements: 'Save Successful', 'Error Saving'.
Body copy. Accepts strings or JSX — ErrorNotification passes a list of <li> elements.
true = red WarningFilled (--theme-error); false = green CheckCircleFilled (--theme-success). The default is the ERROR icon — always pass this argument explicitly.

blocks.title: Toast variants
blocks.intro: One function, three intents. The third argument is the only axis — there is no warning or info toast; use an inline Alert for those.

rows.name: Success
rows.when: An action completed: save, publish, upload, delete.
rows.code: NotificationMessage('Save Successful', '4 record(s) saved successfully', false)

rows.name: Error
rows.when: An action failed and the user should retry or escalate.
rows.code: NotificationMessage('Error Saving', 'Failed to save changes. Please try again.', true)

rows.name: Validation failure
rows.when: antd Form submit fails validation — wire ErrorNotification directly, it formats errorFields into one deduplicated 'Oops!' toast.

blocks.title: ErrorNotification — form validation toast
blocks.caption: The 'Oops!' toast produced by passing antd's onFinishFailed payload to ErrorNotification: one line per distinct field error, duplicate messages dropped.

blocks.title: Canonical toast wiring
blocks.note: ErrorNotification plugs straight into onFinishFailed — no wrapper needed. Submit via GraviButton onClick={() => form.submit()}; htmlType='submit' is on the repo's banned-mistakes list.

blocks.title: antd message — pill states
blocks.caption: Top-center message pills: success, error, warning, and loading states. No title, no body — a single line that earns at most three seconds of attention.

items.rule: Use a NotificationMessage toast for action outcomes — saves, publishes, uploads, deletes.
items.why: Titled, top-right, auto-dismissing: visible without interrupting flow.

items.rule: Use a message pill only for split-second acknowledgements with no title and no consequence.
items.why: If the user might need to act on it, it deserves a toast with a description.

items.rule: Use Modal.confirm before destructive or irreversible actions — never confirm after the fact.
items.why: Toasts report outcomes; they cannot gather consent.

items.rule: Use Overlay to block an in-place region during async work; the content stays mounted and dims.
items.why: Layout holds steady — no flash of empty space when the work finishes.

items.rule: Use LoadingAnimation for first loads where there is nothing to dim yet.
items.why: An empty region with a bare spinner reads as broken; the illustration plus title reads as working.

items.rule: Use an inline antd Alert for warnings that must persist until resolved — validation summaries, range warnings, banner notices.
items.why: Toasts vanish in 4.5 seconds; unresolved problems must not.

blocks.title: Modal.confirm — destructive confirmation
blocks.caption: Confirmation dialog: title states the action as a question, body states the consequence, and the destructive button carries danger styling beside a plain Cancel escape. antd focuses the OK button by default.

blocks.title: Canonical controlled Modal
blocks.note: For one-shot confirmations with no local state, Modal.confirm({ title, content, okText, okButtonProps: { danger: true } }) renders the same dialog imperatively.

blocks.title: Overlay — renderOverlay off and on
blocks.caption: renderOverlay={false} renders children untouched with zero wrapper DOM; renderOverlay={true} wraps them in antd Spin — content dims, the spinner and overlayContent tip center over it.

blocks.title: Overlay
blocks.intro: A conditional antd Spin wrapper. The type accepts all SpinProps, but the implementation reads exactly four props — `spinning`, `size`, and `delay` are ignored.

rows.notes: The region being blocked. Always rendered — dimmed when the overlay is on, bare when off.
Required. true wraps children in <Spin>; false returns children in a Fragment with no extra DOM. Drive it from your loading/saving state.
Label under the spinner — 'Saving…', 'Loading…'. Keep it to one word plus ellipsis.
Custom spinner element. Omit for the standard antd dot spinner.

blocks.title: LoadingAnimation — first-load state
blocks.caption: LoadingAnimation with required animationData, title, and message: Lottie illustration above a left-aligned bold h5 title and p1 message in the empty region.

blocks.title: LoadingAnimation
blocks.intro: Lottie illustration plus Texto title and message. All three content props are required — a bare <LoadingAnimation /> renders nothing visible.

rows.notes: Required. Imported Lottie JSON (e.g. from src/assets/Animation/). There is no built-in default animation.
Required. Bold headline under the animation — 'Loading Quotes'.
Required. Supporting line — 'Fetching the latest quote book…'.
Bumps the type scale to h1 title + h5 message for full-page loads. Default scale is h5 + p1.

blocks.title: Status tokens
blocks.intro: Values shown are the light-theme resolution (ThemeBase/light.less); each theme stylesheet redefines them. Always reference the variable, never the hex.

rows.use: Error tint backgrounds for inline alert surfaces and tags.
Success tint backgrounds for inline alert surfaces and tags.

items.do: Pass NotificationMessage's third argument explicitly: false for success, true for error.
items.dont: Omit it on a success toast.
items.why: showErrorMessage defaults to true — your success copy ships under a red warning icon.

items.do: Gate destructive actions behind Modal.confirm with okButtonProps={{ danger: true }}.
items.dont: Delete on click and announce it with a toast afterward.
items.why: A toast reports an outcome; it cannot collect consent for an irreversible one.

items.do: Wrap a saving or refreshing region in <Overlay renderOverlay={isSaving}>.
items.dont: Hand-roll absolutely-positioned spinner divs over content.
items.why: Spin handles dimming, centering, and pointer blocking consistently; custom overlays drift per page.

items.title: antd v5: `open`, never `visible`
items.detail: A Modal or Drawer given visible={true} simply never appears — no error, no warning in some versions. Every open-state prop in v5 is `open`.

items.title: `destroyOnClose` is deprecated — use `destroyOnHidden`
items.detail: v5 logs a console deprecation warning per render (the demo app currently emits one). Same behavior, new name: unmount modal children when hidden.

items.title: `onVisibleChange` is gone — use `onOpenChange`
items.detail: Popover, Dropdown, and Tooltip all take onOpenChange in v5; the old callback silently never fires. Modal's equivalent is afterOpenChange.

items.title: NotificationMessage defaults to the error icon
items.detail: showErrorMessage defaults to true. NotificationMessage('Saved', '…') renders a red WarningFilled next to success copy. Always pass the third argument.

items.title: Do not copy props from the Feedback showcase page
items.detail: Both showcase specimens render empty: <LoadingAnimation /> is missing the required animationData/title/message, and <Overlay open={true} /> uses a prop that does not exist — the real prop is renderOverlay, with children required.

items.title: NotificationMessage's @deprecated tag is stale
items.detail: The library source flags it 'unused', yet it is the standard toast across the codebase — grid save flows, contract creation, file uploads all call it. Keep using it until a replacement actually ships.
